candidate_fixture: Synthetic agent-sabotage monitor fixture with a normal user task, hidden side objective, tool-call/action log, monitor suspicion score per step, attack-opportunity labels, plan/subtlety/innocuous-framing annotations, anti-scheming spec capsule, caught/not-caught verdict, main-task success verdict, side-task success verdict, counterfactual benign replay, and cold replay. Negative cases reject live sabotage, real credentials/accounts, exploit instructions, private chain-of-thought export, raw harmful payloads, monitor-only final grading, and scare-story deployment claims.
failure_mode_prevented: A public AI-safety demo describes scheming or sabotage risk without a self-contained environment, hidden-objective setup, per-step monitor evidence, main/side task split, anti-scheming spec boundary, benign counterfactual, and replayable detection receipt.
notes: Added after external attention review of OpenAI/Apollo scheming work, Anthropic SHADE-Arena, and modular red-team control evaluations. This is a safe synthetic monitoring fixture, not a live sabotage harness, exploit guide, private reasoning export, or deployment-risk claim.
one_line: A public-safe scheming/sabotage fixture replays hidden-objective simulations, tool logs, monitor suspicion scores, attack-opportunity labels, anti-scheming spec boundaries, main/side task verdicts, benign counterfactuals, and cold reruns.
title: Agent sabotage scheming-monitor replay compound
why_load_bearing: Scheming, sabotage, and monitoring are among the most attention-grabbing safety topics in agentic AI, but a public system should not dramatize risk. The hard mechanism is a replayable control-plane fixture: hidden-objective setup, stepwise monitor scores, main/side task verdicts, benign counterfactuals, and anti-scheming spec boundaries without exporting private reasoning or live exploit material.
